steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the Exterior Angle Theorem
steps.content:
When side NP is extended through P to point T, angle MPT becomes an exterior angle of triangle MNP. By the Exterior Angle Theorem, an exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles (the two angles not at the exterior angle's vertex).

The remote angles are M and N:
$$(x + y) = (3x + 4) + (2x + 9)$$
$$x + y = 5x + 13$$
$$y = 4x + 13$$

steps.title: Apply the Triangle Angle Sum
steps.content:
The three interior angles of triangle MNP must sum to $180^{\circ}$:
$$(3x + 4) + (2x + 9) + (4y + 10) = 180$$
$$5x + 4y + 23 = 180$$
$$5x + 4y = 157$$

steps.title: Solve for x and y
steps.content:
Substitute $y = 4x + 13$ from step 1 into the equation from step 2:
$$5x + 4(4x + 13) = 157$$
$$5x + 16x + 52 = 157$$
$$21x = 105$$
$$x = 5$$
Then $y = 4(5) + 13 = 33$, so $x + y = 5 + 33 = 38$.
